title: Four-friends drink assignment (unique solution)
prompt: Four friends -- Ada, Ben, Cy, Dot -- each ordered a different drink from the set
{tea, cola, water, juice}. Use these clues to determine who ordered what:
1. Ada ordered neither tea nor water.
2. Ben ordered neither cola nor juice.
4. The name of the person who ordered juice comes strictly before the name of
the person who ordered cola in alphabetical order.
There is exactly one assignment consistent with all clues.
Output the solution on a single line, using lowercase drink names, in exactly
this format (person order fixed, no spaces):
Output ONLY that line between <answer> and </answer> tags.
difficulty_rationale: A constraint-satisfaction puzzle requiring elimination (Cy=water, Ben=tea forced) then an alphabetical tiebreak on clue 4 to resolve Ada/Dot. Uniqueness brute-force-verified. Novel content (not a canonical published puzzle), so no memorized answer. Expected to reward the extra reasoning of higher tiers; low may stop at a non-unique guess.
